research_novelty: The extraction map is not defined as a sum; finite additivity and singleton calibration forced atomic mass representation for every coherent finite detector interface.
harder_obstruction_removed: Finite regrouping, ordering, and blocking dependence cannot survive the coherence axioms.
comparison: Mathlib already supplies Finset and finite-sum infrastructure.
This artifact packages a domain-specific detector-coherence uniqueness theorem rather than another direct sum-definition lemma.
The theorem sits below measure-theoretic formalizations such as Haar-measure uniqueness, but uses the same invariant-from-axioms pattern in a finite detector setting.
